entry.title: London Review of Books
entry.summary: London Review of Books published at 2022-07-25 23:32PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Letters
Literary review publishing essay-length book reviews and topical articles on politics, literature, history, philosophy, science and the arts by leading writers and thinkers

entry.title: NY Times Books: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: NY Times Books: 25 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-25 22:51PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Rumrunning, Bootlegging and More Criminal History of ‘Liquor Island’
  • Diana Kennedy’s Complicated Relationship With Mexican Cuisine
  • In Robert Lowell’s ‘Memoirs,’ Mental Illness, Creative Friends and a Takedown of Dad
  • Diana Kennedy, Authority on Mexican Cooking, Dies at 99
  • “Chester Keene Cracks the Code” by Kekla Magoon
  • Review: ‘How to Read Now,’ by Elaine Castillo
  • Geling Yan Says a Movie Fails to Credit Her. The Film World Shrugs.
  • Comics That Read Top to Bottom Are Bringing in New Readers
  • Diana Goetsch on ‘This Body I Wore’
  • That Voice You’re Hearing? It Might Be Julia Whelan.
  • Newly Published, From Maori Myths to Europe’s Eastern Borderlands
  • HarperCollins Workers Strike for Increased Wages, Benefits and Diversity
  • Readers’ Favorite Phantoms, Specters and Chain-Dragging Ghosts
  • After Mocking France’s Literary Elite, a Fraught Invite Into the Club
  • Review: ‘An Honest Living,’ by Dwyer Murphy
  • Review: “The Digital Republic: On Freedom and Democracy in the 21st Century,” Jamie Susskind
  • Review: ‘The Kite Runner’ Trips From Page to Stage
  • Ronni Solbert, Children’s Book Illustrator, Dies at 96
  • 13 New Books We Recommend This Week
  • Review: ‘Fish Swimming in Dappled Water,’ by Riku Onda
  • Michelle Obama Will Publish a New Book, ‘The Light We Carry,’ This Fall
  • Hip, Woke, Cool: It’s All Fodder For the Oxford Dictionary of African American English
  • Gabrielle Zevin Is Not Going to Post This Article
  • Review: ‘Dirt Creek,’ by Hayley Scrivenor and ‘Wake,’ by Shelley Burr
  • Ruth Ware Won’t Read ‘Bleak House’ Until She Retires
The latest book reviews, best sellers, news and features from The NY TImes critics and reporters.

entry.title: Five Books: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • A. J. Jacobs on The Best Puzzle Books
  • Patrick Galbraith on Sense of Place Books
  • Maxine Berg on Global History Books
  • Bahram Rahman on The Best Books about Libraries for 4-8 Year Olds
  • Brenna Hassett on Anthropology Books
  • David Edgerton on The Best Politics Books: the 2022 Orwell Prize for Political Writing
  • Caroline Crampton on The Best Summer Mysteries
  • Noga Arikha on Philosophy, Science and the Body Books
  • Samuel A. Chambers on Money Books
  • Nick Enfield on Language and Post-Truth Books
  • Kevin G. Bethune on Design Books
  • Evan Lieberman on African Politics Books
  • Frank Wynne on The Best of World Literature: The 2022 International Booker Prize Shortlist
  • Myisha Cherry on Anger at Racial Injustice Books
  • Darren Collins on The Best Finance Books for Teens and Young Adults
  • Elizabeth Laird on The Best Historical Fiction: The 2022 Walter Scott Prize Shortlist
  • David George Haskell on Natural History Books
  • Gary Gerstle on Neoliberalism Books
  • Rosa Rankin-Gee on The Best Near-Future Dystopias
  • Cal Flyn on The Notable Novels of Summer 2022
  • Matthew Brookes & Zack Raffin on Wanderlust Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Spring 2022
  • Carole Hillenbrand on The Best History Books: the 2022 Wolfson Prize Shortlist
  • Michele Cobb on The Best Audiobooks: the 2022 Audie Awards
  • Serhii Plokhy on Ukraine and Russia Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Early 2022
  • Cal Flyn on Notable Novels of Spring 2022
  • Jeffrey Archer on The Best Detective Fiction
  • Marion Turner on Best Medieval Historical Fiction
Expert book recommendations

entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 22:56PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Letter: Thomas McKenna on “Moro Warrior”
  • “Thirst” by Varsha Bajaj
  • “Hafez in Love” by Iraj Pezeshkzad
  • “Understanding the India-China Border: The Enduring Threat of War in High Himalaya” by Manoj Joshi
  • Podcast with Kaveh L Hemmat, translator of “The Kushnameh: The Persian Epic of Kush the Tusked” by Iranshah ibn Abu’l-Khayr
  • “Empires of Ideas: Creating the Modern University from Germany to America to China” by William C Kirby
  • “Sensitive Reading: The Pleasures of South Asian Literature in Translation”
  • “Banaras Talkies” by Satya Vyas
  • “An Object of Seduction: Chinese Silk in the Early Modern Transpacific Trade, 1500–1700” by Xiolin Duan
  • “Life Ceremony” by Sayaka Murata
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.
